DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF RICHARD HACK'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART. DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF MAY PROCEED, BUT SHALL BE LIMITED TO NOT MORE THATN 2 HOURS OF PLAINTIFF'S EXAMINATION AND TESTIMONY; AND SHALL BE HELD AT A DATE, TIME AND PLACE TO BE AGREED UPON BY COUNSEL, AND ACCOMODATING PLAINTIFF'S WORK SCHEDULE AND HEALT NEEDS. COMMISSIONER: PAUL SLAVIT. NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Order For Sanctions; Declaration
Off Calendar Aug-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Irving L. Berg, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Richard P. Hack
Marcos Sasso, Esq., appearing by telephone, for Defendant and opposing party: Citibank, N.A. Citi

After hearing, Plaintiff Richard Hack's Motion for protective order is granted in part, denied in part.
Deposition of plaintiff may proceed, but shall be limited to not more thatn 2 hours of plaintiff's examination and testimony; and shall be held at a date, time and place to be agreed upon by counsel, and accomodating plaintiff's work schedule and healt needs. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITIBANK (SOUTH DAKOTA) N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Irving L. Berg, Esq. of The Berg Law Group (415) 924-0742, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Marcos D. Sasso, Esq. of Stroock & Stroock & Lavan (310) 556-5800, Appearing thru CourtCall, for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is overruled. Ten days leave to answer. (302/CWW/FC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
